audit_items.proof_trace: ber_strdup() at fetch.c:71 returns NULL on malloc failure; no NULL check exists before ldap_pvt_hex_unescape(p) is called at fetch.c:80. ldap_pvt_hex_unescape immediately dereferences its argument via `*s` in the for-loop at url.c:1625, causing a NULL pointer dereference (SIGSEGV).
audit_items.attack_scenario: Under memory pressure, an attacker sends a `file://...` URL via LDIF `include:` directives or the URL-fetch API; if ber_strdup fails due to OOM, ldap_pvt_hex_unescape(NULL) crashes the LDAP server process (DoS).
